bibliographic.paper_title: BuildArena: A Physics-Aligned Interactive Benchmark of LLMs for Engineering Construction
bibliographic.authors: Tian Xia, Tianrun Gao, Wenhao Deng, Long Wei, Xiaowei Qian, Yixian Jiang, Chenglei Yu, Tailin Wu

domain_use_case.aec_subdomain: Engineering construction — structural assembly and mechanical design in 3D physics simulation
domain_use_case.intended_user: AI researchers; LLM developers; engineering automation researchers

task.task_type: Physics-grounded construction automation: Transport, Support, Lift
task.task_complexity: Multi-step agentic; 5-6 specialized roles; 6 difficulty dimensions (claimed)
task.output_type: 3D structure files; control sequences; physics simulation metrics (claimed)

modality.notes: Besiege physics simulator; no image/CAD/BIM (claimed)

dataset_properties.n_samples: 9 task-difficulty combinations; 64 simulation runs per model (claimed)
dataset_properties.data_source: Besiege physics simulator (claimed)
dataset_properties.annotation_method: Automated physics simulation outcomes (claimed)
dataset_properties.accessibility: Code at build-arena.github.io; no public dataset package (claimed)
dataset_properties.leakage_risk: Low — interactive simulation (claimed)

evaluation.metrics: Success rate per task-difficulty; task-specific performance indicators; token counts (claimed)
evaluation.baseline_models: GPT-4o, Claude-4 (sonnet-4-20250514), Grok-4, Gemini-2.0-flash, DeepSeek-3.1, Qwen-3, Kimi-K2, Seed-1.6 (claimed)

capability.capability_spans: planning; physical reasoning; tool use

bibliographic.paper_title: AECV-Bench: Benchmarking Multimodal Models on Architectural and Engineering Drawing Understanding
bibliographic.authors: Aleksei Kondratenko, Mussie Birhane, Houssame E. Hsain, Guido Maciocci

domain_use_case.aec_subdomain: Architectural and engineering drawing understanding
domain_use_case.intended_user: Architects, engineers, AEC practitioners, AI/VLM developers

task.task_type: Floor-plan object counting; drawing-grounded document QA (counting, OCR, spatial, comparative) (claimed)
task.task_complexity: Multi-step: symbol recognition → enumeration; spatial inference (claimed)
task.output_type: JSON integer counts; binary correctness 0/1 (claimed)

modality.notes: 2D rasterized PNG; NOT native CAD formats (claimed)

dataset_properties.n_samples: 120 floor plans (counting); 192 QA pairs across 21 drawings (QA) (claimed)
dataset_properties.n_tasks_categories: 2 use cases; 4 object classes; 4 QA categories (claimed)
dataset_properties.data_source: CubiCasa5K, CVC-FP, Apple HQ and Rinker plans, public drawings (claimed)
dataset_properties.annotation_method: Manual counting rules; GPT-5-generated QA; human adjudication (claimed)
dataset_properties.leakage_risk: CubiCasa5K/CVC-FP are public — potential pretraining overlap; not addressed (claimed)

evaluation.metrics: Per-field exact-match accuracy + MAPE (counting); binary correctness, per-category accuracy + LLM-as-judge (QA) (claimed)
evaluation.baseline_models: Gemini 3 Pro, GPT-5.2, Claude Opus 4.5, Grok 4.1 Fast, Amazon Nova 2 Lite, Cohere Command A Vision; GLM-4.6V, Qwen3 VL 8B, NVIDIA Nemotron 12B, Mistral Large 3 (claimed)
evaluation.prompting_method: Single fixed structured prompt; JSON schema enforced (claimed)
evaluation.llm_as_judge_method: LLM-as-judge for binary correctness with human validation (claimed)
evaluation.robustness_tests: Capability gradient: OCR → spatial → symbol counting (claimed)

capability.modality_spans: text; images; engineering drawings; CAD/floor plans
capability.evaluation_spans: exact correctness; accuracy; MAPE/numeric error; LLM-as-judge rubric
capability.capability_spans: OCR/text extraction; perception; spatial reasoning; multimodal grounding

reported_performance.best_model: Object counting: Gemini 3 Pro (mean accuracy 0.51, mean MAPE 16.0%); QA: Gemini 3 (overall accuracy 0.854) [source-confirmed VERIFIER F-075: counting Table 1/Table 2; QA overall 0.854 stated in Fig. 5 only ≈0.85 — figure-read, not tabular]
reported_performance.worst_model: Object counting: Cohere Command A Vision (accuracy 0.29, MAPE 50.8%); QA: Grok 4.1 Fast (0.312) (claimed)
reported_performance.average_performance: Models plateau 0.4–0.55 exact-match for counting; strong models ~0.85 on QA (claimed)
reported_performance.performance_by_task: Text-labeled categories (bedrooms, toilets) far outperform graphical (doors, windows) (claimed)
reported_performance.reported_failure_modes: Door/window hallucination; difficulty in dense regions; OCR > spatial > symbol enumeration gap (claimed)
reported_performance.practical_interpretation: Current VLMs have significant gap in AEC drawing understanding, especially symbol-level object recognition vs. text-based categories (claimed)

bibliographic.paper_title: BridgeEQA: Virtual Embodied Agents for Real Bridge Inspections
bibliographic.authors: Subin Varghese, Joshua Gao, Asad Ur Rahman, Vedhus Hoskere

domain_use_case.aec_subdomain: Infrastructure inspection — bridge inspection; civil engineering asset management
domain_use_case.intended_user: Bridge inspectors, structural engineers, AI/VLM researchers

task.task_type: Embodied QA: bridge condition assessment, visual reasoning, spatial navigation, image citation (claimed)
task.task_complexity: Multi-step embodied; multi-scale reasoning; 47+ image sequences per scene (claimed)
task.output_type: Open-ended text; numerical condition ratings 0-9; image citations (claimed)

modality.notes: RGB egocentric imagery from bridge inspection walks (~47.93 images/scene; 200 scenes) (claimed)

dataset_properties.n_samples: 2,200 QA pairs; 200 bridge scenes; ~9,586 total images (claimed)
dataset_properties.n_tasks_categories: 4 question subtypes: Comparative, Relational, Integrative, Spatial (claimed)
dataset_properties.data_source: 200 Vermont Agency of Transportation (VTrans) inspection reports (claimed)
dataset_properties.annotation_method: Gemini 2.5 Flash/Pro zero-shot QA generation; human review 100-pair sample (claimed)
dataset_properties.expert_validation: Human review 100% accuracy; RAGAS Faithfulness 0.997, Answer Relevancy 0.997 (claimed)
dataset_properties.leakage_risk: Not addressed; VTrans reports may be online (claimed)

evaluation.metrics: Condition Rating Accuracy (exact + ±1); Image Citation Relevance (VLM-as-judge); Answer Correctness (LLM-as-judge) (claimed)
evaluation.baseline_models: Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite, Gemini 2.5 Flash, Grok 4 Fast; scene graph + EMVR variants (claimed)
evaluation.prompting_method: Bridge inspection context; EMVR MDP-based image selection (claimed)

capability.capability_spans: perception; spatial reasoning; retrieval grounding; multimodal grounding

reported_results.metric_name: Answer Correctness — Socratic LLM w/ SG
Answer Correctness — Socratic LLM w/ SG
Answer Correctness — Socratic LLM w/ SG
Image Citation Relevance — Socratic LLM w/ SG
Image Citation Relevance — Multi-Frame w/ SG
Image Citation Relevance — EMVR SG Only
Image Citation Relevance — Socratic LLM w/ SG
Image Citation Relevance — Multi-Frame w/ SG
Image Citation Relevance — EMVR SG Only
Image Citation Relevance — Socratic LLM w/ SG
Image Citation Relevance — Multi-Frame w/ SG
Image Citation Relevance — EMVR SG Only

reported_performance.best_model: Grok 4 Fast with EMVR (w/ Images + Scene Graph): Answer Correctness 0.648, Image Citation Relevance 0.889, +9.34 pp condition rating accuracy vs baseline [source-confirmed VERIFIER F-075]
reported_performance.worst_model: Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite Multi-Frame: Answer Correctness 0.507, Image Citation Relevance 0.717 (claimed)
reported_performance.performance_by_task: Comparative / Relational / Integrative / Spatial breakdown reported (claimed)
reported_performance.reported_failure_modes: Poor image citations (irrelevant citations → incorrect answers); hallucinated citations for non-existent images (claimed)
reported_performance.human_baseline: Expert inspectors 98% consistency ±1 condition rating (upper bound reference) (claimed)
reported_performance.practical_interpretation: EMVR outperforms direct VLM approaches; retrieval bottleneck is key challenge; 50% answer correctness gap vs expert inspectors (claimed)

bibliographic.paper_title: ArchPlanVQA: Benchmark and Comprehensive Evaluation of VLMs for Understanding Architectural Floor Plan CAD Drawings

task.output_type: Number, Boolean, String — 3 answer formats (claimed)

modality.notes: CAD floor plan images (PNG rendered from FloorPlanCAD); questions in Chinese (claimed)

dataset_properties.n_samples: 128 QA entries confirmed in repo; full dataset size [VERIFY — ASCE paywall] (claimed)
dataset_properties.data_source: FloorPlanCAD dataset (10,000+ real-world floor plans) via SymPoint pipeline (claimed)
dataset_properties.annotation_method: Custom QA pairs; LLM-assisted synonym mapping; full method [VERIFY] (claimed)
dataset_properties.license: [VERIFY — no license file in GitHub repo]
dataset_properties.accessibility: Public GitHub + Google Drive dataset link (claimed)
dataset_properties.train_test_split: Three-way split inherited from FloorPlanCAD; exact ratios [VERIFY] (claimed)
dataset_properties.leakage_risk: [VERIFY — paper paywalled]

evaluation.metrics: Number accuracy (exact match); Boolean accuracy; String accuracy (LLM synonym matching); Overall accuracy (claimed)
evaluation.baseline_models: GPT-4o, Gemini 1.5 Pro, Claude 3.7 Sonnet, Qwen2.5-VL-72B-Instruct (claimed)

bibliographic.paper_title: DrafterBench: Benchmarking Large Language Models for Tasks Automation in Civil Engineering
bibliographic.authors: Yinsheng Li, Zhen Dong, Yi Shao

domain_use_case.aec_subdomain: Civil engineering drawing revision and construction document management
domain_use_case.intended_user: Civil engineers, drafters, automation tool developers

task.task_type: LLM-agent task automation: 12 task types (Text/Table/Vector × Adding/Revise/Mapping/Refresh) (claimed)
task.task_complexity: Multi-step agentic; 64 difficulty combinations (claimed)
task.output_type: Programmatic tool execution via 46 custom functions (claimed)

modality.notes: Text-only structured JSON drawing data + text instructions; no image/vision (claimed)

dataset_properties.data_source: 100+ real-world drawing revision files from design firms and construction companies (claimed)
dataset_properties.annotation_method: Human verification for solvability and value distribution (claimed)
dataset_properties.expert_validation: Yes — human verification by domain practitioners (claimed)
dataset_properties.leakage_risk: Not discussed (claimed)

evaluation.metrics: Average Task Score; Comprehensive Score; 6 subtask dimensions (claimed)
evaluation.baseline_models: OpenAI o1, ChatGPT-4o-2024-08-06, Claude 3.5 Sonnet, DeepSeek-v3-685B, Qwen2.5-72B, Llama3-70B (claimed)
evaluation.robustness_tests: 6 difficulty dimension analysis; vague vs precise specification ablation (claimed)

capability.evaluation_spans: task success; tool-call correctness; code/drawing-edit correctness
capability.capability_spans: planning; tool use; domain knowledge

examples.text: Text-only task: natural language instruction to insert a structured table (3 rows × 3 columns, Arial 12pt) into a specified rectangle on page 3 of a civil engineering PDF, with ground truth as Python code using fitz/PDFbf libraries.
examples.instruction: In the file 'AB12CD.pdf', on page 3, within the second rectangle, add a table with the following content: first row 'Beam, Length, Material'; second row 'B1, 5m, Steel'; third row 'B2, 7m, Concrete'; set the font to 'Arial' and font size to 12.
examples.ground_truth_format: Python code (fitz + PDFbf) — programmatic PDF table insertion

domain_use_case.aec_subdomain: Multi-domain AEC knowledge: architecture, construction management, structural/civil, codes/regulatory (11+ domains covered) (claimed)

task.task_type: Domain-knowledge QA; exam reasoning across 23 tasks (claimed)
task.task_complexity: Memorization / understanding / reasoning / calculation / application; LLM-as-judge rubric (claimed)

evaluation.baseline_models: DeepSeek-R1, QwQ-32B, and others (claimed)
evaluation.robustness_tests: Task-level breakdown: memorization / understanding / reasoning / calculation / application (claimed)

reported_performance.best_model: DeepSeek-R1: best in 14/23 tasks; 96.15% on MEP Calculation (Task 4-2) [source-confirmed VERIFIER F-075]
reported_performance.average_performance: Wide variance by task; memorization tasks >90%; application tasks often <50%; creation tasks <60% across all models (claimed)
reported_performance.performance_by_task: Full 23-task × 9-model table available in paper (all values cited as claimed)
reported_performance.reported_failure_modes: Tabular data (Tasks 2-3, 3-3 all models drastic underperformance); compliance checking (Task 5-1-3); brand compliance (5-1-4); knowledge-application gap; professional document generation <60% threshold (claimed)
reported_performance.practical_interpretation: Reasoning models (DeepSeek-R1, QwQ-32B) outperform standard LLMs on calculation/application; standard LLMs outperform reasoning models on proofreading; all models fail at complex creation tasks (claimed)

bibliographic.paper_title: FloorplanQA: A Benchmark for Spatial Reasoning in LLMs using Structured Representations
bibliographic.authors: Fedor Rodionov, Abdelrahman Eldesokey, Michael Birsak, John Femiani, Bernard Ghanem, Peter Wonka

domain_use_case.aec_subdomain: Spatial reasoning over floor plans
domain_use_case.intended_user: Spatial AI researchers, architects, LLM developers

task.task_type: Spatial reasoning: distance, visibility, path-finding, placement feasibility (claimed)
task.task_complexity: Exact/structured answer correctness over symbolic floor plans (claimed)
task.output_type: Exact answers; MAPE for numeric outputs (claimed)

modality.notes: TEXT ONLY — JSON/XML encoded 2D floorplans; NO image modality (claimed)

dataset_properties.data_source: Algorithmically generated floor plans; 1,800/2,000 synthetic (claimed)
dataset_properties.annotation_method: Algorithmic generation — strong leakage control (d8=2) (claimed)
dataset_properties.leakage_risk: Strong structural safeguard: algorithmic generation separates train/test (claimed)

evaluation.metrics: Exact correctness; MAPE/numeric error (claimed)
evaluation.baseline_models: Multiple models including open-weight (claimed)

capability.capability_spans: spatial reasoning; quantitative reasoning

examples.text: Question template (no released dataset — no specific floor plan or computed ground-truth value): asks the model to calculate Euclidean distance in meters between centroids of two named objects (fridge and stove) from a symbolic floor plan representation.
examples.modality: text-only (structured symbolic floor plan + NL question; NO raster image)
examples.question: Calculate the Euclidean distance in meters between the centroids of the fridge and the stove
examples.answer_format: N (numeric scalar; layout-specific value programmatically derived from symbolic JSON — no fixed ground truth in paper table)

examples.text: Question template (no released dataset — no specific floor plan or boolean ground truth): asks the model to check whether a 2m×3m desk table can fit inside a room without overlaps, derived from a symbolic floor plan representation.
examples.modality: text-only (structured symbolic floor plan + NL question; NO raster image)
examples.question: Check if a 2m × 3m desk table can fit fully inside the room without overlaps
examples.answer_format: B (boolean Yes/No; programmatically derived from symbolic JSON — no fixed ground truth in paper table)

reported_performance.best_model: GPT-OSS-120B on Pair Distance (Kitchens): 99.3% [source-confirmed VERIFIER F-075]
reported_performance.average_performance: Reasoning models generally outperform general models; wide variance by question type (claimed)
reported_performance.performance_by_task: Pair Distance generally higher; Max Box and Repositioning harder; detailed per-type per-room breakdown available (claimed)
reported_performance.reported_failure_modes: Constraint violations, centroid errors, clearance errors, token truncation for reasoning models (claimed)
reported_performance.practical_interpretation: Spatial reasoning over symbolic floor plans remains challenging; best models near-perfect on distance but fail on space optimization (claimed)

bibliographic.paper_title: Are Large Pre-trained Vision Language Models Effective Construction Safety Inspectors?
bibliographic.authors: Xuezheng Chen, Zhengbo Zou

domain_use_case.aec_subdomain: Construction management — site safety compliance and visual inspection
domain_use_case.intended_user: Construction safety managers, site supervisors, AI researchers

task.task_type: Compliance checking; embodied inspection: safety rule VQA + visual grounding (claimed)

bibliographic.paper_title: VL-Con: Vision-Language Dataset for Deep Learning-based Construction Monitoring Applications
bibliographic.authors: [VERIFY — Shun-Hsiang Hsu, Junryu Fu, Mani Golparvar-Fard]
bibliographic.venue: Proceedings of the 41st ISARC 2024, Lille, France

domain_use_case.aec_subdomain: Construction monitoring and site management
domain_use_case.intended_user: Researchers developing VLMs for construction domain; AEC practitioners evaluating VLM adaptation

task.task_type: Zero-shot image classification; construction activity recognition; VLM domain adaptation
task.task_complexity: Single-hop (image-level classification)
task.output_type: Classification label (0=incorrect, 1=incorrect but construction-related, 2=correct)

modality.notes: Image-text pairs; image-level labels; no video, BIM, or 3D (claimed)

dataset_properties.n_samples: ~3,363 refined/labeled images; 710 manually web-collected images (142 unambiguous activities ×5) (claimed)
dataset_properties.n_tasks_categories: 641 construction activities (ASTM Uniformat II); 142 unambiguous activities in curated subset (claimed)
dataset_properties.data_source: LAION-5B (retrieved via CLIP embedding similarity) + manual web collection (claimed)
dataset_properties.annotation_method: Automated CLIP-based construction semantic predictor + prompt engineering; manual human verification for subset (claimed)
dataset_properties.expert_validation: Partial — manually web-collected subset reviewed by researchers; automated annotations not expert-validated (claimed)
dataset_properties.accessibility: Public — GitHub (https://github.com/huhuman/VL-Con); images via Google Drive or web links
dataset_properties.train_test_split: No formal benchmark evaluation splits; VL-Con is primarily training/adaptation corpus (claimed)
dataset_properties.leakage_risk: High — LAION-5B images likely overlap with CLIP and other VLM pretraining data; leakage not controlled (claimed)

evaluation.metrics: Accuracy, Top-3 Accuracy on BCS dataset (claimed)
evaluation.baseline_models: CLIP (ViT-based, zero-shot; evaluated on BCS dataset to characterize domain gap) (claimed)

capability.capability_spans: perception; multimodal grounding; domain knowledge

reported_results.note: NOTE: VL-Con is a DATASET CONSTRUCTION paper, not a multi-VLM leaderboard. Results reflect CLIP image retrieval quality and a ResNet-18 construction semantic filter — NOT head-to-head VLM evaluation.

reported_performance.best_model: CLIP zero-shot image retrieval on BCS dataset: 37.7% correct (prompt "{activity}"), 36.4% correct (prompt "{activity} in construction"); ResNet-18 construction semantic filter 73.63% validation accuracy [source-confirmed VERIFIER F-075 against ISARC 2024 proceedings doi:10.22260/ISARC2024/0146, Table 1 p.1130 + body p.1132; Tier-3 caveat: dataset-construction paper, CLIP retrieval + ResNet-18 filter, NOT a multi-VLM leaderboard]
reported_performance.average_performance: Domain gap documented between LAION-pretrained CLIP and construction-specific imagery; VL-Con proposed to bridge this gap (claimed)
reported_performance.reported_failure_modes: Performance degradation of VLMs on construction-specific visual concepts due to domain gap with LAION general-domain pretraining (claimed)
reported_performance.practical_interpretation: VL-Con enables VLM domain adaptation for construction monitoring; ASTM Uniformat II taxonomy provides structured coverage of AEC activities (claimed)

bibliographic.paper_title: BIM-Edit: Benchmarking Large Language Models for IFC-Based Building Information Modeling
bibliographic.authors: [VERIFY]
bibliographic.repo_url: [VERIFY — GitHub repo mentioned in paper but exact URL not confirmed from arXiv HTML]

domain_use_case.aec_subdomain: Building Information Modeling (BIM) — IFC file editing and manipulation
domain_use_case.intended_user: BIM managers, structural/MEP engineers, architectural designers, AEC AI researchers

task.task_type: Structured BIM editing — create, update, and delete IFC elements from natural language instructions
task.task_complexity: High — requires understanding IFC schema, spatial topology, property hierarchies; 3 instruction phrasings (direct/spatial/topological) per edit
task.output_type: Modified IFC file (evaluated across geometric + semantic + topological axes)

modality.notes: Text (natural language instructions) + IFC structured data (ISO 16739 Building Information Model files). FILLS BIM/IFC 0/17 MODALITY GAP — first benchmark in this inventory requiring LLMs to read/write IFC-format structured building models. (claimed)

dataset_properties.n_tasks_categories: 3 edit operations (create/update/delete) × 3 instruction types (direct/spatial/topological) × 2 complexity levels (simple: 162, complex: 162); derived from 47 IFC models (11 realistic + 36 synthetic) (claimed)
dataset_properties.data_source: 47 IFC models — controlled edits authored in Blender (Bonsai add-on) and Autodesk Revit; each edit verified by a second independent author (claimed)
dataset_properties.annotation_method: Human expert authoring + second-author verification; professional BIM tools (Blender/Bonsai + Revit) used for controlled ground-truth generation (claimed)
dataset_properties.expert_validation: Second-author cross-check of all 324 tasks; Revit used to confirm validity of IFC edits (claimed)
dataset_properties.accessibility: Public — HuggingFace: https://huggingface.co/BIM-Edit; GitHub repository with cached LLM outputs (claimed)
dataset_properties.train_test_split: Evaluation-only (no training set); 324 tasks all in benchmark evaluation corpus (claimed)
dataset_properties.leakage_risk: Low — IFC editing tasks are highly domain-specific; unlikely to appear verbatim in pretraining; no explicit contamination testing (claimed)

evaluation.metrics: Geometry Score (bidirectional Chamfer distance, normalized); Semantic Score (IFC class + property agreement); Topology Score (F1 for node/edge edits, λ=0.3); Composite Score (unweighted mean) (claimed)
evaluation.baseline_models: Gemini 3.0 Flash, Qwen 3.6 Plus, Claude Sonnet 4.6, GPT-5.4 Pro, GPT-5.4 Mini, DeepSeek V3.2, Gemma 4 31B (claimed)
evaluation.prompting_method: 3 instruction variants per edit: direct / spatial / topological (claimed)
evaluation.zero_few_shot: [VERIFY — likely zero-shot per standard LLM evaluation]
evaluation.robustness_tests: 3 instruction phrasings per edit implicitly test robustness to language variation; results reported by instruction type (claimed)

capability.task_spans: BIM editing; IFC manipulation; structured building model understanding
capability.evaluation_spans: geometric accuracy; semantic agreement; topological correctness; composite score
capability.capability_spans: structured data understanding; spatial reasoning; IFC schema comprehension; instruction following

reported_performance.average_performance: All 7 models below 50% composite; highest perfect-task rate (≥98% on all axes): Qwen 3.6 Plus at 3.4% [claimed]
reported_performance.performance_by_task: Geometry Score highest (~40–69% across models); Semantic + Topology substantially lower (~30–48%); create tasks typically easier than delete/update [claimed]
reported_performance.reported_failure_modes: High within-model variance (SD ~32–37pp); topological and semantic understanding weaker than geometric manipulation; complex scenes significantly harder than simple [claimed]
reported_performance.practical_interpretation: IFC BIM editing remains largely unsolved for current LLMs; geometric manipulation more tractable than semantic/topological constraints [claimed]
